assessments.rationale: Port MV feeders often saturated near quayside under OPS demand (cruise up to 16 MW). Standard DSO upgrade process applies; BESS itself can relieve grid bottleneck.
assessments.mitigation: Early DSO/TSO grid-capacity study (Netzanschlussanfrage) at site selection, Size BESS as grid-relief buffer where feeder saturated, Coordinate with port authority master-plan MV expansion programme

assessments.rationale: Terminals are dense near quay but port authorities control ample peripheral land. 40-80 m²/MWh fits within outer yards and unused logistics zones.
assessments.mitigation: Modular container placement in outer-yard or peripheral terminal areas, Baugenehmigung via port master plan (faster than greenfield), Vertical stacking or 40ft HC containers to reduce footprint

assessments.rationale: Ports carry ISPS + dangerous-goods regulatory overlay. Standard VdS clearance design adapts with port-fire authority coordination — well-established path, no novel rule.
assessments.mitigation: Site-specific fire concept with port Feuerwehr and VdS, Mass-explosive safety distance to bunkering and dangerous-goods zones, Separation walls for clusters >3 MWh, Coordination with ISPS security perimeter

assessments.rationale: Thermal runaway risk is inherent but fully contained by base product suppression system. Port proximity to flammables requires only placement-distance check, not new hardware.
assessments.mitigation: Standard NOVEC 1230 suppression + deflagration venting (base product), Mass-explosive-zone distance analysis to fuel bunkering, Coordination with dedicated port fire brigade

assessments.rationale: VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 certification required for any German MV plant — included in base EPC scope. No port-specific delta.
assessments.mitigation: FGW TR8 type B plant certificate (standard EPC scope), Parallel certification with procurement and civil, DSO acceptance included in project timeline

assessments.rationale: Ports are critical infrastructure (NIS2 / EU CER). Public-procurement + port operators may require EU-origin cells or enhanced supplier scrutiny, increasing BoM cost 10-25% but not blocking.
assessments.mitigation: EU-origin or dual-sourced LFP cells where port operator requires, Supplier due-diligence under NIS2 / EU CER critical-infrastructure framework, CRMA and EU Battery Regulation compliance documentation

assessments.rationale: German coastal ports operate −10 to +35 °C year-round — well within the −20 to +50 °C envelope. Salt-air ingress is a separate enclosure-protection topic (IP55+ / stainless), not an ambient-range breach.
10-15% AC-AC loss is a structural property of the technology, not a market-specific barrier. Priced into OPEX and arbitrage economics for every use case.

AFIR Reg. 2023/1804 mandate by 2030 + EU/BMWK funding programmes + port-authority creditworthiness mean ports are mandate-driven buyers, not payback-gated like C&I. €250-400/kWh threshold comfortably met.
